# RaidScout — Full Documentation for AI Agents

## Overview

RaidScout is a web-based guild operations platform for competitive MMO guilds. It supports any game via custom server creation, pre-seeded templates (LordNine: Infinite Class with 39 bosses), and flexible configuration. The platform handles boss spawn tracking, multi-guild kill rotation, DKP auction bidding, AI-powered rally screenshot scanning, loot distribution, gear tracking, combat power monitoring, attendance, leaderboards, and Discord bot integration.

## Architecture

- **Frontend**: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite + TanStack Query v4 + Tailwind CSS
- **Backend**: Supabase (PostgreSQL, Auth, Realtime, Edge Functions, Storage)
- **Discord Bot**: Node.js, esbuild, Fly.io deployment, Discord WebSocket Gateway
- **Staging**: `aavobydtkonccgyfxrmw` (Supabase project)
- **Production**: `cjuacehmienztxrhwnlg` (Supabase project)

## Server Access Model

Users create or join servers. Each server has:
- **Owner**: Full control (settings, members, billing)
- **Moderator**: Partial control (configurable permissions)
- **Member**: Claimed profile access (view bosses, bid, view gear)
- **Viewer**: Read-only access via invite key

Members claim their in-game profile by name. Staff approve/decline claims. Unlink available to undo wrong claims.

## DKP Auction System

- `dkp_auctions` table: Separate auction concept from items. Each auction row has `item_id`, `dkp_cost`, `bid_end_time`, `guild_id`, `quantity`, `status` (active/cancelled/resolved).
- `dkp_bids` table: Bids linked to `auction_id` and `member_id`. Statuses: active/won/lost/cancelled.
- `dkp_transactions` table: DKP ledger (earn_kill, earn_adjustment, earn_refund, spend_bid).
- `dkp_balances` view: Aggregated balance per member per server.
- Key RPCs: `mark_item_for_bid`, `place_bid`, `resolve_auction`, `auto_resolve_auction`, `get_active_auctions`, `get_item_bids`, `get_resolved_bids`.
- Soft-close: Bids in last 3 minutes extend auction by 3 minutes.
- Auto-resolve: Discord bot cron resolves expired auctions, picking highest bidder.

## Key Database Tables

- `servers`: Server config with game, timezone, subscription, invite_code
- `members`: Member profiles with user_id, discord_user_id, guild_id, class, combat_power
- `guilds`: Guild names per server
- `bosses`: Boss definitions with spawn_type, timer_hours, schedule, rotation_type
- `death_records`: Kill history with killer_id, boss_id, death_time
- `boss_guilds`: Guild rotation assignments per boss
- `dkp_auctions`, `dkp_bids`, `dkp_transactions`: DKP system
- `member_claim_requests`: Profile claim system
- `server_members`: Server membership with role (owner/moderator/member)
- `notifications`: User notifications with type, title, body, metadata
- `items`: Item catalog with name, image_url, rarity, game/server_id

## Discord Bot Commands

- `!spawn`, `!kill`, `!forcespawn`, `!forcespawnall`, `!party`
- `!dkp`, `!dkp top`, `!mybids`, `!bidstatus`
- `!notifhere`, `!cmdhere`, `!threadhere`
- `!updatestats`, `!editstats`, `!commands`
- Multi-prefix support, custom aliases, timezone-aware
